description: Unique request identifier — not used in rule logic
Request creation timestamp — converted externally to days_until_required
Submission channel (email / portal / chat) — not used in rule logic
Language of request text — not used in rule logic
Requesting business unit — not used in rule logic
Free-text request description — pre-processed externally
Test scenario classification tags — not used in rule logic
Unique supplier identifier — part of supplier identity; not available to rule logic
Supplier display name — part of supplier identity; not available to rule logic
Country of supplier headquarters — part of supplier identity; not available to rule logic
Semicolon-separated ISO-2 codes of regions the supplier serves — used for delivery-country pre-filtering only; not available to rule logic
True if the supplier is restricted and excluded before rule evaluation — surviving suppliers always have is_restricted=False so rules must not reference this field
Human-readable restriction rationale — informational only; not available to rule logic
Free-text supplier notes — informational only; not available to rule logic
L1 procurement category (IT / Facilities / Professional Services / Marketing)
L2 procurement subcategory (e.g. Laptops / Cloud Compute / Office Chairs)
Total budget amount available for this request
Budget currency — EUR / CHF / USD
Number of units or service days requested
Unit of measurement for quantity (e.g. devices / hours / days) — required to evaluate day-based category thresholds such as CR-007
Target delivery or service country (ISO-2 code e.g. DE / CH / US)
Calendar days from today until the required delivery or service start date — pre-computed from required_by_date
Supplier name explicitly stated by the requester — null if none mentioned
Current incumbent supplier for this category and requester — null if none
True if the request involves data that must remain within a specific jurisdiction
True if the requester has stated ESG or sustainability requirements
Supplier quality score 0–100 from supplier master data — higher is better; used as a primary rank input
Supplier risk score 0–100 from supplier master data — lower is better; high values can trigger SRM penalties or ER-006 escalation
Supplier ESG / sustainability score 0–100 from supplier master data — higher is better; relevant when esg_requirement is True
True if this supplier appears on the organisation's preferred supplier list for this category
True if the supplier can fulfil data-residency requirements for this category and region — cross-referenced with request-level data_residency_constraint
Maximum units (devices / hours / service days) the supplier can deliver per calendar month — used to detect single-supplier capacity risk (ER-006)
Current contract status with this supplier — active / expired / pilot; rules should treat non-active suppliers as lower priority
Supplier pricing model — tiered / usage / subscription / day_rate / fixed / performance / blended_rate; used in category-specific rules (e.g. CR-007 requires day_rate for consultants)
True if the supplier's service regions include the request's delivery country — computed during supplier pre-filtering and always True for suppliers that reach rule evaluation
Supplier ranking score — higher value means higher priority in the shortlist; one value per supplier in the supplier matrix
Minimum number of compliant supplier quotes required by the applicable approval threshold rule (AT-001 through AT-015)
True if category and value rules permit fast-track single-quote approval without a standard competitive process (CR-003)
True if a security architecture review is mandatory before award (CR-005)
True if an engineering or CAD compatibility review is mandatory before award (CR-002)
True if a business design sign-off is required before award (CR-006)
True if named consultant CVs or equivalent capability profiles must be provided before award (CR-007)
True if the supplier must demonstrate relevant certifications or references before award (CR-008)
True if a brand safety review is mandatory before final award (CR-010 / ER-007)
True if SEM performance baseline or benchmark assumptions must be included in the proposal (CR-009)
True if requester clarification is needed before sourcing can continue — missing information (ER-001)
True if Procurement Manager approval or deviation approval is required — preferred supplier restricted or mid-value threshold (ER-002 / AT-002 / AT-007 / AT-012)
True if Head of Category escalation is required — no compliant supplier found or high-value threshold (ER-004 / AT-003 / AT-008 / AT-013)
True if Head of Strategic Sourcing approval is required — very high value contracts (ER-003 / AT-004 / AT-009 / AT-014)
True if CPO approval is required — highest value contracts (AT-005 / AT-010 / AT-015)
True if Security and Compliance Review is required — data residency constraint conflict (ER-005)
True if Sourcing Excellence Lead escalation is required — single-supplier capacity risk (ER-006)
True if Marketing Governance Lead review is required — brand safety concerns (ER-007)
True if Regional Compliance Lead approval is required — supplier not registered or sanction-screened in delivery country (ER-008)
